The term \u2018qualid\u2019 is already d.documentation:
The "qualid" was previously used in a "Extract Foreign Constant"
   command. Using "Extract Inlined Constant" for "qualid" would
   override this command.

Extract Foreign Constant is supported on.documentation: Foreign function calls are only supported for OCaml.

This error is thrown if "qualid" is of sort "Type" as external
   functions only work for functions.

The term \u2018qualid\u2019 is already d.documentation:
The "qualid" was previously used in a "Extract Inlined Constant"
   command. Using "Extract Foreign Constant" for "qualid" would
   override this command.

Extract Callback is supported only for O.documentation:
The callback registration mechanism "Callback.register" is specific
   to OCaml. Thus, the command is only usable when extracting OCaml
   code.

Nothing to rewrite.documentation: The strategy didn't find any matches.

No progress made.documentation: If the strategy succeeded but made no progress.

Unable to satisfy the rewriting constrai.documentation:
If the strategy succeeded and made progress but the corresponding
   rewriting constraints are not satisfied.

\u2018qualid\u2019 is already a coercion.documentation: "qualid" is already registered as a coercion.

Funclass cannot be a source class.documentation:
Funclass as a source class is currently not supported. This may
   change in the future.

\u2018qualid\u2019 is not a function.documentation: "qualid" is not a function, so it cannot be used as a coercion.

Cannot find the source class of \u2018qu.documentation: Rocq can not infer a valid source class.

Cannot recognize \u2018coercion_class\u2.documentation:
The inferred source class of the coercion differs from the one
   specified.

Cannot find the target class.documentation:
The target class of the coercion is not specified and cannot be
   inferred. Make sure that the target is not a variable.

Found target class \u2018coercion_class\.documentation:
The inferred target class of the coercion differs from the one
   specified.

Non extensible universe declaration not.documentation:
The absence of additional universes or constraints cannot be
   properly enforced even without Program.

Ill-formed recursive definition.documentation:
This error can happen when one tries to define a function by
   structural recursion on a subset object, which means the Rocq
   function looks like:

      Program Fixpoint f (x : A | P) := match x with A b => f b end.

   Supposing "b : A", the argument at the recursive call to "f" is not
   a direct subterm of "x" as "b" is wrapped inside an "exist"
   constructor to build an object of type "{x : A | P}".  Hence the
   definition is rejected by the guardedness condition checker.
   However one can use wellfounded recursion on subset objects like
   this:

      Program Fixpoint f (x : A | P) { measure (size x) } :=
        match x with A b => f b end.

   One will then just have to prove that the measure decreases at each
   recursive call. There are three drawbacks though:

      1. A measure function has to be defined;

      2. The reduction is a little more involved, although it works
         well using lazy evaluation;

      3. Mutual recursion on the underlying inductive type isn’t
         possible anymore, but nested mutual recursion is always
         possible.

Not a valid ring equation.documentation:
The conclusion of the goal is not provable in the corresponding
   ring theory.

Arguments of ring_simplify do not have a.documentation:
"ring_simplify" cannot simplify terms of several rings at the same
   time. Invoke the tactic once per ring structure.

Cannot find a declared ring structure ov.documentation:
No ring has been declared for the type of the terms to be
   simplified. Use "Add Ring" first.

Cannot find a declared ring structure fo.documentation: Same as above in the case of the "ring" tactic.

Bad ring structure.documentation:
The proof of the ring structure provided is not of the expected
   type.

Bad lemma for decidability of equality.documentation:
The equality function provided in the case of a computational ring
   has not the expected type.

Ring operation should be declared as a m.documentation:
A setoid associated with the carrier of the ring structure has been
   found, but the ring operation should be declared as morphism. See
   Tactics enabled on user provided relations.

The cumulative attribute can only be use.documentation:
Using this attribute requires being in a polymorphic context, i.e.
   either having the "Universe Polymorphism" flag on, or having used
   the "universes(polymorphic)" attribute as well.

Polymorphic sorts can only be declared i.documentation:
Generated when a "Sort" command is issued outside a section with
   universe polymorphism set. Either scope the "Sort" with an
   enclosing "Section" or be explicit about creating a global sort by
   turning universe polymorphism off.

Cannot declare global sort qualities ins.documentation: The "Sort" command is not supported inside module types.

Template-polymorphism and universe polym.documentation:
This attribute cannot be used in a full universe polymorphic
   context, i.e. if the "Universe Polymorphism" flag is on or if the
   "universes(polymorphic)" attribute is used.

There is no flag, option or table with t.documentation:
This error message is raised when calling the "Test" command
   (without the "for" clause), or the "Print Table" command, for an
   unknown "setting_name".

There is no qualid-valued table with thi.documentation:
These error messages are raised when calling the "Add" or "Remove"
   commands, or the "Test" command with the "for" clause, if
   "setting_name" is unknown or does not have the right type.

There is no string-valued table with thi.documentation:
These error messages are raised when calling the "Add" or "Remove"
   commands, or the "Test" command with the "for" clause, if
   "setting_name" is unknown or does not have the right type.

\u2018ident\u2019 already exists. (Theor.documentation:
The name you provided is already defined. You have then to choose
   another name.

Nested proofs are discouraged and not al.documentation:
You are asserting a new statement when you're already in proof
   mode. This feature, called nested proofs, is disabled by default.
   To activate it, turn the "Nested Proofs Allowed" flag on.

Non strictly positive occurrence of \u20.documentation:
The types of the constructors have to satisfy a *positivity
   condition* (see Section Positivity Condition). This condition
   ensures the soundness of the inductive definition. Positivity
   checking can be disabled using the "Positivity Checking" flag or
   the "bypass_check(positivity)" attribute (see Controlling Typing
   Flags).

The conclusion of \u2018type\u2019 is no.documentation:
The conclusion of the type of the constructors must be the
   inductive type "ident" being defined (or "ident" applied to
   arguments in the case of indexed inductive types — cf. next
   section).

No field named \u2018ident\u2019 in \u20.documentation:
Raised when the final "ident" in the left-hand side "qualid" of a
   "with_declaration" is applied to a module type "qualid" that has no
   field named this "ident".

Error: \"where\" clause not supported fo.documentation:
"where" clauses are only supported for "record_field"s, not for the
   overall record definition.

Records declared with the keyword Record.documentation:
The record name "ident" appears in the type of its fields, but uses
   the "Record" command. Use  the "Inductive" or "CoInductive" command
   instead.

\u2018ident\u2019 already exists.documentation: The fieldname "ident" is already defined as a global.

Argument \u2018name\u2019 is a trailing.documentation:
For instance:

      Fail Definition double [n] := n + n.
          The command has indeed failed with message:
          Argument n is a trailing implicit, so it can't be declared non maximal.
          Please use { } instead of [ ].

Cannot infer a term for this placeholder.documentation: Rocq was not able to deduce an instantiation of a “_”.

The constructor \u2018ident\u2019 expect.documentation:
Patterns are incorrect (because constructors are not applied to the
   correct number of arguments, because they are not linear or they
   are wrongly typed).

The variable ident is bound several time.documentation:
Patterns are incorrect (because constructors are not applied to the
   correct number of arguments, because they are not linear or they
   are wrongly typed).

Found a constructor of inductive type te.documentation:
Patterns are incorrect (because constructors are not applied to the
   correct number of arguments, because they are not linear or they
   are wrongly typed).

Non exhaustive pattern matching.documentation: The pattern matching is not exhaustive.

The elimination predicate term should be.documentation:
The elimination predicate provided to match has not the expected
   arity.

Unable to infer a match predicate.documentation:
There is a type mismatch between the different branches. The user
   should provide an elimination predicate.

Either there is a type incompatibility o.documentation:
There is a type mismatch between the different branches. The user
   should provide an elimination predicate.

The path for Rocq libraries is wrong.documentation:
Unless "-boot" was passed, Rocq fails with this error if
   "$libdir/theories/Init/Prelude.vo" does not exist.

The path for Rocq plugins is wrong.documentation:
Unless "-boot" was passed, Rocq fails with this error if
   "$runtimelib/plugins" does not exist.

Could not find package rocq-runtime.documentation:
Rocq fails with this error if OCamlfind package "rocq-runtime"
   could not be found.

Cannot simultaneously select shelved and.documentation:
This error occurs if you try to select both shelved goals and
   focused goals in the same selector list, e.g. by doing "1, [A]:
   tactic" when "A" is shelved. To work around this error, first
   "Unshelve" the desired goals.

No matching clauses for match.documentation:
For at least one of the focused goals, there is no branch that
   matches its pattern *and* gets at least one success for
   "ltac_expr".

Argument of match does not evaluate to a.documentation: This happens when "ltac_expr_term" does not denote a term.

No matching clauses for match goal.documentation:
No clause succeeds, i.e. all matching patterns, if any, fail at the
   application of the "ltac_expr".

Notation levels must range between 0 and.documentation:
The level of a notation must be an integer between 0 and 6
   inclusive.

Unbound [value|constructor] X.documentation:
* if "X" is meant to be a term from the current static environment,
     replace the problematic use by "'X".

   * if "X" is meant to be a hypothesis from the local context,
     replace the problematic use by "&X".

The reference X was not found in the cur.documentation:
* if "X" is meant to be a tactic expression bound by a Ltac2 let or
     function, replace the problematic use by "$X".

   * if "X" is meant to be a hypothesis from the local context,
     replace the problematic use by "&X".

Incorrect number of tactics (expected N.documentation: This error can occur when using the version with a "do" clause.

No such binder.documentation: "natural" is 0 or more than the number of unbound variables.

No such bound variable \u2018ident\u2019.documentation:
The specified binder name "ident_1" is not used in the "one_term".
   "ident_2 ..." lists all the valid binder names.

Not the right number of missing argument.documentation:
Generated when the first form of "bindings" doesn't have the
   expected number of arguments.

Cannot infer a term for this placeholder.documentation:
There is a hole in the term you gave whose type cannot be inferred.
   Put a cast around it.

Unable to unify \u2018one_term\u2019 wit.documentation:
The "apply" tactic failed to match the conclusion of "one_term".
   You can help "apply" by transforming your goal with the "change" or
   "pattern" tactics.

Unable to apply lemma of type \"...\" on.documentation:
This happens if the conclusion of "ident" does not match any of the
   premises of the type of "one_term".

Unable to find an instance for the varia.documentation:
This occurs when some instantiations of the premises of "one_term"
   are not deducible from the unification. This is the case, for
   instance, when you want to apply a transitivity property.  To fix
   this, add bindings for the "ident"s using to "with bindings" or use
   "eapply".

\u2018ident\u2019 is already used.documentation: The provided "ident" is already used in the *local context*.

No product even after head-reduction.documentation:
There is nothing to introduce even after "hnf" has been completely
   applied.

No quantified hypothesis named \u2018ide.documentation:
The "ident" in the "until" clause doesn't appear as a *dependent
   premise*.

No \u2018natural\u2019-th non dependent.documentation: There are fewer than "natural" premises in the goal.

The term \"\u2018type\u2019\" has type \.documentation:
Occurs when the argument "type" (in the first form) or "one_type"
   (in the third form) is not of type "Prop", "Set" nor "Type".

Proof is not complete. (assert).documentation: "ltac_expr3" was not able to prove the new hypothesis.

Module/section \u2018qualid\u2019 not fo.documentation:
There is no constant in the environment named "qualid", where
   "qualid" is in an "inside" or "outside" clause.

Cannot find library foo in loadpath.documentation:
The command did not find the file foo.vo. Either foo.v exists but
   is not compiled or foo.vo is in a directory which is not in your
   *load path*.

Required library \u2018qualid\u2019 matc.documentation:
Either the file to load must be required with a more discriminating
   suffix (at worst, with its full logical name) or there is an error
   in the configuration (command line arguments or environment
   variables).

Compiled library \u2018ident\u2019.vo ma.documentation:
The command tried to load library file "ident".vo that depends on
   some specific version of library "qualid" which is not the one
   already loaded in the current Rocq session. Probably "ident.v" was
   not properly recompiled with the last version of the file
   containing module "qualid".

Bad magic number.documentation:
The file "ident.vo" was found but either it is not a Rocq compiled
   module, or it was compiled with an incompatible version of Rocq.

The file \u2018ident\u2019.vo contains l.documentation:
The library "qualid_2" is indirectly required by a "Require". The
   *load path* maps "qualid_2" to "ident.vo", which was compiled using
   a load path that bound it to "qualid_1".  Usually the appropriate
   solution is to recompile "ident.v" using the correct *load path*.

File not found on loadpath: \u2018string.documentation:
"findlib" is not able to find the plugin name. Possible reasons
   are:

   * The plugin does not exist or is misspelled. You can get the list
     of available libraries by running "ocamlfind list | grep coq".

   * The metadata for "findlib" has not been set properly (see above).

Dynlink error: execution of module initi.documentation:
The plugin declaration in some ".mlg" file does not match the
   "findlib" plugin name. In the example of "rocq-
   runtime.plugins.ltac", one has to write "DECLARE PLUGIN "rocq-
   runtime.plugins.ltac"".

shared library failed: Coq Error: \u2018.documentation:
The plugin declaration in some ".mlg" file does not match the
   "findlib" plugin name. In the example of "rocq-
   runtime.plugins.ltac", one has to write "DECLARE PLUGIN "rocq-
   runtime.plugins.ltac"".

plugin name anymore. Plugins should be l.documentation:
The plugin declaration in some ".mlg" file does not match the
   "findlib" plugin name. In the example of "rocq-
   runtime.plugins.ltac", one has to write "DECLARE PLUGIN "rocq-
   runtime.plugins.ltac"".

public name according to findlib, for ex.documentation:
The plugin declaration in some ".mlg" file does not match the
   "findlib" plugin name. In the example of "rocq-
   runtime.plugins.ltac", one has to write "DECLARE PLUGIN "rocq-
   runtime.plugins.ltac"".

package-name.foo and not foo_plugin.documentation:
The plugin declaration in some ".mlg" file does not match the
   "findlib" plugin name. In the example of "rocq-
   runtime.plugins.ltac", one has to write "DECLARE PLUGIN "rocq-
   runtime.plugins.ltac"".

Invalid backtrack.documentation: The user wants to undo more commands than available in the history.

The command has not failed!.documentation:
If the given "command" succeeds, then "Fail sentence" fails with
   this error message.

The type \u2018ident\u2019 must be regis.documentation:
The type must be defined with "Primitive" command before this
   "Primitive" command (declaring an operation using the type) will
   succeed.

The \"at\" syntax isn't available yet fo.documentation: Appears when there is an "at" clause on the conclusion.

\u2018qualid\u2019 cannot be used as a h.documentation:
The head symbol of the type of "qualid" is a bound variable such
   that this tactic cannot be associated with a constant.

I don\u2019t know how to handle dependen.documentation:
The decision procedure managed to find a proof of the goal or of a
   discriminable equality but this proof could not be built in Rocq
   because of dependently-typed functions.

Goal is solvable by congruence but some.documentation:
The decision procedure could solve the goal with the provision that
   additional arguments are supplied for some partially applied
   constructors. Any term of an appropriate type will allow the tactic
   to successfully solve the goal. Those additional arguments can be
   given to congruence by filling in the holes in the terms given in
   the error message, using the "with" clause.

Cannot recognize a boolean equality.documentation:
The goal is not of the form "t = u". Especially note that "btauto"
   doesn't introduce variables into the context on its own.

Found no subterm matching \u2018term\u20.documentation:
This happens if "term" does not occur in, respectively, the named
   hypothesis or the goal.

This happens if "term" does not occur in, respectively, the named
   hypothesis or the goal.

Section variable \u2018ident\u2019 occur.documentation:
Raised when the variable is a section variable with indirect
   dependencies in the goal. If "ident" is a section variable, it must
   not have any indirect occurrences in the goal, i.e. no global
   declarations implicitly depending on the section variable may be
   present in the goal.

Raised when the variable is a section variable with indirect
   dependencies in the goal. If "ident" is a section variable, it must
   not have any indirect occurrences in the goal, i.e. no global
   declarations implicitly depending on the section variable may be
   present in the goal.

Cannot turn [inductive|constructor] into.documentation:
Occurs when trying to unfold something that is defined as an
   inductive type (or constructor) and not as a definition.

   Example:

      Goal 0 <= 1.

          1 goal
            
            ============================
            0 <= 1

      unfold le.
          Toplevel input, characters 7-9:
          > unfold le.
          >        ^^
          Error: Cannot coerce le to an evaluable reference.

\u2018ident\u2019 is opaque.documentation:
Raised if you are trying to unfold a definition that has been
   marked opaque.

   Example:

      Opaque Nat.add.

      Goal 1 + 0 = 1.

          1 goal
            
            ============================
            1 + 0 = 1

      unfold Nat.add.
          Toplevel input, characters 0-14:
          > unfold Nat.add.
          > ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
          Error: Nat.add is opaque.

   Error Bad occurrence number of qualid.

   Error qualid does not occur.

The reference \u2018qualid\u2019 was not.documentation: There is no constant named "qualid" in the environment.

No focused proof (No proof-editing in pr.documentation:
You tried to use a proof mode command such as "Qed" outside of
   proof mode.

All is a predefined collection containin.documentation:
When issuing a "Proof using" command, **All** used as a collection
   name always means "use all variables".

This proof is focused, but cannot be unf.documentation:
You are trying to use "}" but the current subproof has not been
   fully solved.

Brackets do not support multi-goal selec.documentation:
Brackets are used to focus on a single goal given either by its
   position or by its name if it has one.

Wrong bullet \u2018bullet\u2019: Current.documentation:
Before using bullet "bullet_1" again, you should first finish
   proving the current focused goal. Note that "bullet_1" and
   "bullet_2" may be the same.

Wrong bullet \u2018bullet\u2019: Bullet.documentation:
You must put "bullet_2" to focus on the next goal. No other bullet
   is allowed here.

No such goal. Focus next goal with bulle.documentation:
You tried to apply a tactic but no goals were under focus. Using
   "bullet" is  mandatory here.

No such goal. Try unfocusing with }.documentation:
You just finished a goal focused by "{", you must unfocus it with
   "}".

Cannot recognize a statement based on \u.documentation:
The type of the "induction_arg" (in an "induction_clause") must
   reduce to the "reference" which was inferred as the type the
   induction principle operates on. Note that it is not enough to be
   convertible, but you can work around that with "change":

      Definition N := nat.

          N is defined

      Axiom strong : forall P, (forall n:N, (forall m:N, m < n -> P m) -> P n)
        -> forall n, P n.

          strong is declared


      Axiom P : N -> Prop.

          P is declared


      Goal forall n:nat, P n.

          1 goal
            
            ============================
            forall n : nat, P n

      intros.

          1 goal
            
            n : nat
            ============================
            P n

      Fail induction n using strong.

          The command has indeed failed with message:
          Cannot recognize a statement based on N.

      change N in n.

          1 goal
            
            n : N
            ============================
            P n

      (* n is now of type N, matching the inferred type that strong operates on *)
      induction n using strong.
          1 goal
            
            n : N
            H : forall m : N, m < n -> P m
            ============================
            P n

Unable to find an instance for the varia.documentation: Use the "with bindings" clause or the "einduction" tactic instead.

Not a negated primitive equality.documentation:
When "induction_arg" is not provided, the goal must be in the form
   "term <> term".

Nothing to inject.documentation: Generated when one side of the equality is not a constructor.

Type of \u2018ident\u2019 is not an equa.documentation:
When applied to a hypothesis, "inversion_sigma" can only handle
   equalities of the listed sigma types.

\u2018ident\u2019 is not an equality of.documentation:
When applied to a hypothesis, "inversion_sigma" can only be called
   on hypotheses that are equalities using "Stdlib.Logic.Init.eq".

Unterminated string in notation.documentation:
Occurs when the notation string contains an unterminated quoted
   string, as e.g. in "Reserved Notation "A ""an unended string B"",
   for which the user may instead mean "Reserved Notation "A ""an
   ended string"" B".

End of quoted string not followed by a s.documentation:
Occurs when the notation string contains a quoted string which
   contains a double quote not ending the quoted string, as e.g. in
   "Reserved Notation "A ""string""! B"" or "Reserved Notation "A
   ""string""!"" B"", for which the user may instead mean "Reserved
   Notation "A ""string"""" ! B", "Reserved Notation "A
   ""string""""!"" B", or "Reserved Notation "A '""string""!' B".

Unexpected only printing for an only par.documentation:
Cannot enable or disable for printing a notation that was
   originally defined as only parsing.

Unexpected only parsing for an only prin.documentation:
Cannot enable or disable for parsing a notation that was originally
   defined as only printing.

More than one interpretation bound to th.documentation:
Use "all" to allow enabling or disabling multiple notations in a
   single command.

Unknown custom entry.documentation: In "in custom ident", "ident" is not a valid custom entry name.

No notation provided.documentation:
At least one of "string", "qualid", "one_term" or "scope_name" must
   be provided.

\u2018string\u2019 cannot be interpreted.documentation:
Occurs when "Print Notation" can't find a notation associated with
   "string". This can happen, for example, when the notation does not
   exist in the current context, "string" is not specific enough,
   there are missing spaces between symbols, or some symbols need to
   be quoted with ""'"".

Unknown custom entry: \u2018ident\u2019.documentation:
Occurs when "Notation" or "Print Notation" can't find the custom
   entry given by the user.

Scope names should not start with an und.documentation:
Scope names starting with an underscore would make the "%_" syntax
   ambiguous.

Scope delimiters should not start with a.documentation:
Scope delimiters starting with an underscore would make the "%_"
   syntax ambiguous.

'via' and 'abstract' cannot be used toge.documentation:
With the "abstract after" option, the parser function
   "qualid_parse" does not reduce large numbers to a normal form,
   which prevents doing the translation given in the "mapping" list.

Cannot interpret this number as a value.documentation:
The number notation registered for "type" does not support the
   given number. This error is given when the interpretation function
   returns "None" (when "qualid_type" is "option qualid_type") or
   "Error e" (when "qualid_type" is "result qualid_type _", in which
   case "e" will be printed and appended to the error message), or if
   the interpretation is registered only for integers or non-negative
   integers, and the given number has a fractional or exponent part or
   is negative.

overflow in int63 literal \u2018bigint\u.documentation:
The constant's absolute value is too big to fit into a 63-bit
   integer "PrimInt63.int".

\u2018qualid\u2019 should go from Number.documentation:
The parsing function given to the "Number Notation" command is not
   of the right type.

\u2018qualid\u2019 should go from \u2018.documentation:
The printing function given to the "Number Notation" command is not
   of the right type.

Unexpected term \u2018term\u2019 while p.documentation:
Parsing functions must always return ground terms, made up of
   function application, constructors, inductive type families, sorts
   and primitive integers.  Parsing functions may not return terms
   containing axioms, bare (co)fixpoints, lambdas, etc.

Unexpected non-option term \u2018term\u2.documentation:
Parsing functions expected to return an "option" must always return
   a concrete "Some" or "None" when applied to a concrete number
   expressed as a (hexa)decimal.  They may not return opaque
   constants.

Multiple 'via' options.documentation: At most one "via" option can be given.

Multiple 'warning after' or 'abstract af.documentation:
At most one "warning after" or "abstract after" option can be
   given.

Cannot interpret this string as a value.documentation:
The string notation registered for "type" does not support the
      given string.  This error is given when the interpretation
      function returns "None" or "Error e".

   Error qualid_parse should go from Byte.byte, (list Byte.byte), or PrimString.string to type or (option type).

      The parsing function given to the "String Notation" command is
      not of the right type.

   Error qualid_print should go from type to T or (option T), where T is either Byte.byte, (list Byte.byte), or PrimString.string.

      The printing function given to the "String Notation" command is
      not of the right type.

   Error Unexpected term term while parsing a string notation.

      Parsing functions must always return ground terms, made up of
      function application, constructors, inductive type families,
      sorts, primitive integers and primitive strings.  Parsing
      functions may not return terms containing axioms, bare
      (co)fixpoints, lambdas, etc.

   Error Unexpected non-option term term while parsing a string notation.

      Parsing functions expected to return an "option" must always
      return a concrete "Some" or "None" when applied to a concrete
      string expressed as a decimal.  They may not return opaque
      constants.

\u2018qualid\u2019 should go from Byte.b.documentation:
The parsing function given to the "String Notation" command is not
   of the right type.

\u2018qualid\u2019 should go from \u2018.documentation:
The printing function given to the "String Notation" command is not
   of the right type.

Unexpected term \u2018term\u2019 while p.documentation:
Parsing functions must always return ground terms, made up of
   function application, constructors, inductive type families, sorts,
   primitive integers and primitive strings.  Parsing functions may
   not return terms containing axioms, bare (co)fixpoints, lambdas,
   etc.

Unexpected non-option term \u2018term\u2.documentation:
Parsing functions expected to return an "option" must always return
   a concrete "Some" or "None" when applied to a concrete string
   expressed as a decimal.  They may not return opaque constants.

\u2018type\u2019 is not an inductive typ.documentation:
String and number notations can only be declared for inductive
   types. Declare string or numeral notations for non-inductive types
   using "number_string_via".

\u2018qualid\u2019 was already mapped to.documentation: Duplicates are not allowed in the "mapping" list.

Missing mapping for constructor \u2018qu.documentation: A mapping should be provided for "qualid" in the "mapping" list.

Cannot interpret in \u2018scope_name\u20.documentation:
The inductive type used to register the string or number notation
   is no longer available in the environment.  Most likely, this is
   because the notation was declared inside a functor for an inductive
   type inside the functor.  This use case is not currently supported.

   Alternatively, you might be trying to use a primitive token
   notation from a plugin which forgot to specify which module you
   must "Require" for access to that notation.

Syntax error: [prim:reference] expected.documentation:
The type passed to "String Notation" or "Number Notation" must be a
   single qualified identifier.

Both functions passed to "String Notation" or "Number Notation"
   must be single qualified identifiers.

\u2018qualid\u2019 is bound to a notatio.documentation:
Identifiers passed to "String Notation" or "Number Notation" must
   be global references, or notations which evaluate to single
   qualified identifiers.

Cannot find inversion information for hy.documentation:
This error may be raised when some inversion lemma failed to be
   generated by Function.

\u2018qualid\u2019 does not respect the.documentation:
The test for ambiguous coercion paths may yield false positives
   involving the coercion "qualid". Use the "warnings" attribute with
   "-uniform-inheritance" to silence this warning.

New coercion path ... is ambiguous with.documentation:
The check for ambiguous paths failed. The paths for which this
   check fails are displayed by a warning in the form "[f₁;..;fₙ] : C
   >-> D".

   The convertibility checking procedure for coercion paths is
   complete for paths consisting of coercions satisfying the *uniform
   inheritance condition*, but some coercion paths could be reported
   as ambiguous even if they are convertible with existing ones when
   they have coercions that don't satisfy this condition.

... is not definitionally an identity fu.documentation:
If a coercion path has the same source and target class, that is
   said to be circular. When a new circular coercion path is not
   convertible with the identity function, it will be reported as
   ambiguous.

Bad relevance.documentation:
This is a developer warning, which is treated as an error by
   default. It is emitted by the kernel when it is passed a term with
   incorrect relevance marks. This is always caused by a bug in Rocq
   (or a plugin), which should thus be reported and fixed. In order to
   allow the user to work around such bugs, we leave the ability to
   unset the "bad-relevance" warning for the time being, so that the
   kernel will silently repair the proof term instead of failing.

\u2018ident\u2019 is already declared as.documentation: This command has no effect when used on a typeclass.

Ignored instance declaration for \u201c\.documentation:
Using the "::" syntax in the "record_definition" or "constructor"
   with a right-hand-side that is not itself a Class has no effect
   (apart from emitting this warning).

Using inferred default mode: \u201cmode\.documentation:
Indicates that the "mode" for a "Class" declaration has been
   assigned automatically using the default mode. This warning is
   named "class-declaration-default-mode". It is disabled by default.
   Enable it to find (and fix) any typeclasses that don't have
   explicit mode declarations.

Automatically declaring \u2018ident\u201.documentation:
Warning "auto-template" can be used (it is off by default) to find
   which types are implicitly declared template polymorphic by "Auto
   Template Polymorphism".

   An inductive type can be forced to be template polymorphic using
   the "universes(template)" attribute: in this case, the warning is
   not emitted.

no-template-universe.documentation:
The attribute was used but the inductive definition does not
   satisfy the criterion to be template polymorphic.

Use of \"Variable\" or \"Hypothesis\" ou.documentation:
Warning generated when using "Variable" or its equivalent instead
   of "Local Parameter" or its equivalent. This message is an error by
   default, it may be convenient to disable it while debuging.

This command does not support this attri.documentation:
This warning is configured to behave as an error by default.  You
   may turn it into a normal warning by using the "Warnings" option:

      Set Warnings "unsupported-attributes".

      #[ foo ] Comments.
          Toplevel input, characters 3-6:
          > #[ foo ] Comments.
          >    ^^^
          Warning: This command does not support this attribute: foo.
          [unsupported-attributes,parsing,default]
          Comments ok

There is no flag or option with this nam.documentation:
This warning message can be raised by "Set" and "Unset" when
   "setting_name" is unknown.  It is a warning rather than an error
   because this helps library authors produce Rocq code that is
   compatible with several Rocq versions. To preserve the same
   behavior, they may need to set some compatibility flags or options
   that did not exist in previous Rocq versions.

Cannot import local constant, it will be.documentation:
This warning is printed when a name in the list of names to import
   was declared as a local constant, and the name is not imported.

\u2018ident\u2019 cannot be defined beca.documentation:
The type of the projection "ident_1" depends on previous
   projections which themselves could not be defined.

\u2018ident\u2019 cannot be defined.documentation:
The projection cannot be defined.  This message is followed by an
   explanation of why it's not possible, such as:

   1. The *body* of "ident" uses an incorrect elimination for "ident"
      (see "Fixpoint" and Destructors).

\u2018ident\u2019 cannot be defined beca.documentation:
The projection for the named field "ident_field" can't be defined.
   For example, "Record R:Prop := { f:nat }" generates the message "f
   cannot be defined ... and R is not".  Records of sort "Prop" must
   be non-informative (i.e. indistinguishable).  Since "nat" has
   multiple inhabitants, such as "{| f := 0 |}" and "{| f := 1 |}",
   the record would be informative and therefore the projection can't
   be defined.

Ignoring implicit binder declaration in.documentation:
This is triggered when setting an argument implicit in an
   expression which does not correspond to the type of an assumption
   or to the *body* of a definition. Here is an example:

      Definition f := forall {y}, y = 0.
          Toplevel input, characters 24-25:
          > Definition f := forall {y}, y = 0.
          >                         ^
          Warning: Ignoring implicit binder declaration in unexpected position.
          [unexpected-implicit-declaration,syntax,default]
          f is defined

Making shadowed name of implicit argumen.documentation:
This is triggered when two variables of same name are set implicit
   in the same block of binders, in which case the first occurrence is
   considered to be unnamed. Here is an example:

      Check let g {x:nat} (H:x=x) {x} (H:x=x) := x in 0.
          Toplevel input, characters 0-50:
          > Check let g {x:nat} (H:x=x) {x} (H:x=x) := x in 0.
          > ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
          Warning: Making shadowed name of implicit argument accessible by position.
          [shadowed-implicit-name,syntax,default]
          let g := fun (x : nat) (H : x = x) (x0 : ?A@{x0:=x}) (_ : x0 = x0) => x0 in 0
               : nat
          where
          ?A : [x0 : nat  H : x0 = x0  x : ?A |- Type] (x cannot be used)

Unused variable \u2018ident\u2019 might.documentation:
This indicates that an unused pattern variable "ident" occurs in a
   pattern-matching clause.

   The warning can be deactivated by using a variable name starting
   with "_" or by setting "Set Warnings "-unused-pattern-matching-
   variable"".

   Here is an example where the warning is activated.

   Example:

      Definition is_zero (o : option nat) := match o with
      | Some _ => true
      | x => false
      end.
          Toplevel input, characters 71-72:
          > Definition is_zero (o : option nat) := match o with | Some _ => true | x => false end.
          >                                                                        ^
          Warning: Unused variable x might be a misspelled constructor. Use _ or _x to
          silence this warning. [unused-pattern-matching-variable,default]
          is_zero is defined

Ltac Profiler encountered an invalid sta.documentation:
Currently, "reset ltac profile" is not very well-supported, as it
   clears all profiling information about all tactics, including ones
   above the current tactic.  As a result, the profiler has trouble
   understanding where it is in tactic execution.  This mixes
   especially poorly with backtracking into multi-success tactics.  In
   general, non-top-level calls to "reset ltac profile" should be
   avoided.

SSReflect: cannot obtain new equations o.documentation:
The tactic was run on an equation that cannot generate simpler
   equations, for example "x = 1".

Duplicate clear of H. Use { }H instead o.documentation:
The warning can be silenced or made fatal with the "Warnings"
   option with the "duplicate-clear" key.

Require inside a module is deprecated an.documentation:
Note that the "Import" and "Export" commands can be used inside
   modules.

   See also: Chapter The Rocq Prover commands

File ... found twice in ....documentation:
The file is found in more than once in the top directories
   associated with the given "dirpath". In this case the first
   occurrence is selected.

Use of the non-reference term \u2018term.documentation:
Any non-reference term passed in a "using" clause is ignored. We
   recommend adding such hints to the context via the "pose proof"
   tactic instead. For backwards compatibility, we still parse any
   term in "using" clause for the time being, but you should consider
   removing them.

This hint is not local but depends on a.documentation:
A hint with a non-local attribute was added inside a section, but
   it refers to a local variable that will go out of scope when
   closing the section. As a result the hint will not survive either.

\u2018ident\u2019 is both name of a Coll.documentation:
If a specified name is ambiguous (it could be either a "Collection"
   or a "Variable"), then it is assumed to be a "Collection" name.

Variable All is shadowed by Collection n.documentation: This is variant of the previous warning for the **All** collection.

New Collection definition of \u2018ident.documentation: Redefining a "Collection" overwrites the previous definition.

\u2018ident\u2019 was already a defined.documentation:
The "Proof using" command allows specifying both "Collection" and
   "Variable" names. In case of ambiguity, a name is assumed to be
   Collection name.

register-all.documentation:
The "All" and "AllForall" predicate need to be defined and
   registered before the definition of the nested inductive type. They
   are not generated on the fly if they are not found. If they are not
   registered, no induction hypothesis is created for the nested
   argument.

   When the nesting is done using an inductive, it is possible to
   generate them using "Scheme All".

closed-notation-not-level-0.documentation:
It is usually better to put closed notations, that is the ones
   starting and ending with a terminal symbol, at level 0.

level-0-notation-not-closed.documentation:
Notations at level 0 should be closed, since there is no next level
   for associativity.

postfix-notation-not-level-1.documentation:
It is usually better to put postfix notations, that is the ones
   ending with a terminal symbol, at level 1.

notation-incompatible-prefix.documentation:
The two notations have a common prefix but different levels. The
   levels of one of the notations should be adjusted to match the
   other. See factorization for details.

Found no matching notation to enable or.documentation: No previously defined notation satisfies the given constraints.

Activation of abbreviations does not exp.documentation: "in custom" and "in constr" are not compatible with abbreviations.
Scopes are not compatible with abbreviations.

Use of \u2018string\u2019 Notation is de.documentation:
This warning is disabled by default to avoid spurious diagnostics
   due to legacy notation in the Rocq standard library. It can be
   turned on with the "-w disj-pattern-notation" flag.

Stack overflow or segmentation fault hap.documentation:
When a "Number Notation" is registered in the current scope with
   "(warning after bignat)", this warning is emitted when parsing a
   number greater than or equal to "bignat".

To avoid stack overflow, large numbers i.documentation:
When a "Number Notation" is registered in the current scope with
   "(abstract after bignat)", this warning is emitted when parsing a
   number greater than or equal to "bignat". Typically, this indicates
   that the fully computed representation of numbers can be so large
   that non-tail-recursive OCaml functions run out of stack space when
   trying to walk them.

The 'abstract after' directive has no ef.documentation:
As noted above, the "(abstract after natural)" directive has no
   effect when "qualid_parse" lands in an "option" type.

\u2018type\u2019 was already mapped to \.documentation:
Two pairs in the "mapping" list associate types that might be
   incompatible.

Type of \u2018qualid\u2019 seems incompa.documentation:
A mapping given in the "mapping" list associates a constant with a
   seemingly incompatible constructor.

Cannot define graph for \u2018ident\u201.documentation:
The generation of the graph relation ("R_ident") used to compute
   the induction scheme of ident raised a typing error. Only "ident"
   is defined; the induction scheme will not be generated. This error
   happens generally when:

   * the definition uses pattern matching on dependent types, which
     "Function" cannot deal with yet.

   * the definition is not a *pattern matching tree* as explained
     above.

Cannot define principle(s) for \u2018ide.documentation:
The generation of the graph relation ("R_ident") succeeded but the
   induction principle could not be built. Only "ident" is defined.
   Please report.

Cannot build functional inversion princi.documentation: "functional inversion" will not be available for the function.

Library File \u2018qualid\u2019 is depre.documentation:
"qualid" or "string" is the notation, "string_since" is the version
   number, "string_note" is the note (usually explains the
   replacement).

   Explicitly "Require"ing a file that has been deprecated, using the
   "Attributes" command, triggers a "Library File" deprecation
   warning. Requiring a deprecated file, even indirectly through a
   chain of "Require"s, will produce a "Library File (transitively
   required)" deprecation warning if the "Warnings" option
   "deprecated-transitive-library-file" is set (it is "-deprecated-
   transitive-library-file" by default, silencing the warning).

Library File (transitively required) \u2.documentation:
"qualid" or "string" is the notation, "string_since" is the version
   number, "string_note" is the note (usually explains the
   replacement).

   Explicitly "Require"ing a file that has been deprecated, using the
   "Attributes" command, triggers a "Library File" deprecation
   warning. Requiring a deprecated file, even indirectly through a
   chain of "Require"s, will produce a "Library File (transitively
   required)" deprecation warning if the "Warnings" option
   "deprecated-transitive-library-file" is set (it is "-deprecated-
   transitive-library-file" by default, silencing the warning).

Ltac2 alias \u2018qualid\u2019 is deprec.documentation:
"qualid" or "string" is the notation, "string_since" is the version
   number, "string_note" is the note (usually explains the
   replacement).

   Explicitly "Require"ing a file that has been deprecated, using the
   "Attributes" command, triggers a "Library File" deprecation
   warning. Requiring a deprecated file, even indirectly through a
   chain of "Require"s, will produce a "Library File (transitively
   required)" deprecation warning if the "Warnings" option
   "deprecated-transitive-library-file" is set (it is "-deprecated-
   transitive-library-file" by default, silencing the warning).

"qualid" or "string" is the notation, "string_since" is the version

   replacement).

   Explicitly "Require"ing a file that has been deprecated, using the
   "Attributes" command, triggers a "Library File" deprecation
   warning. Requiring a deprecated file, even indirectly through a
   chain of "Require"s, will produce a "Library File (transitively
   required)" deprecation warning if the "Warnings" option
   "deprecated-transitive-library-file" is set (it is "-deprecated-
   transitive-library-file" by default, silencing the warning).

Ltac2 notation \u2018ltac2_syntax_class\.documentation:
"qualid" or "string" is the notation, "string_since" is the version
   number, "string_note" is the note (usually explains the
   replacement).

   Explicitly "Require"ing a file that has been deprecated, using the
   "Attributes" command, triggers a "Library File" deprecation
   warning. Requiring a deprecated file, even indirectly through a
   chain of "Require"s, will produce a "Library File (transitively
   required)" deprecation warning if the "Warnings" option
   "deprecated-transitive-library-file" is set (it is "-deprecated-
   transitive-library-file" by default, silencing the warning).

Ltac2 constructor \u2018qualid\u2019 is.documentation:
"qualid" or "string" is the notation, "string_since" is the version
   number, "string_note" is the note (usually explains the
   replacement).

   Explicitly "Require"ing a file that has been deprecated, using the
   "Attributes" command, triggers a "Library File" deprecation
   warning. Requiring a deprecated file, even indirectly through a
   chain of "Require"s, will produce a "Library File (transitively

   "deprecated-transitive-library-file" is set (it is "-deprecated-
   transitive-library-file" by default, silencing the warning).

Notation \u2018string\u2019 is deprecate.documentation:
"qualid" or "string" is the notation, "string_since" is the version
   number, "string_note" is the note (usually explains the
   replacement).

   Explicitly "Require"ing a file that has been deprecated, using the
   "Attributes" command, triggers a "Library File" deprecation
   warning. Requiring a deprecated file, even indirectly through a
   chain of "Require"s, will produce a "Library File (transitively
   required)" deprecation warning if the "Warnings" option
   "deprecated-transitive-library-file" is set (it is "-deprecated-
   transitive-library-file" by default, silencing the warning).

Tactic \u2018qualid\u2019 is deprecated.documentation:
"qualid" or "string" is the notation, "string_since" is the version
   number, "string_note" is the note (usually explains the
   replacement).

   Explicitly "Require"ing a file that has been deprecated, using the
   "Attributes" command, triggers a "Library File" deprecation
   warning. Requiring a deprecated file, even indirectly through a
   chain of "Require"s, will produce a "Library File (transitively

   "deprecated-transitive-library-file" is set (it is "-deprecated-

Tactic Notation \u2018qualid\u2019 is de.documentation:
"qualid" or "string" is the notation, "string_since" is the version
   number, "string_note" is the note (usually explains the
   replacement).

   Explicitly "Require"ing a file that has been deprecated, using the
   "Attributes" command, triggers a "Library File" deprecation
   warning. Requiring a deprecated file, even indirectly through a
   chain of "Require"s, will produce a "Library File (transitively

   "deprecated-transitive-library-file" is set (it is "-deprecated-
   transitive-library-file" by default, silencing the warning).

\u2018string\u2019.documentation:
"string_note" is the note. It's common practice to start it with a
   capital and end it with a period.

   Explicitly "Require"ing a file that has a warn message set using
   the "Attributes" command, triggers a "warn-library-file" warning.
   Requiring such a file, even indirectly through a chain of
   "Require"s, will produce a "warn-transitive-library-file" warning
   if the "Warnings" option "warn-transitive-library-file" is set (it
   is "-warn-transitive-library-file" by default, silencing the
   warning).
